content.content:
1→# Variety and Agent Design
     2→
     3→## Reference: Cybernetics Applied to AI Agent Architecture
     4→
     5→**Lineage:** Ashby (theory) → Beer (engineering discipline) → applied to human-agent system design
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Core Concepts
    10→
    11→**Variety** is the count of distinguishable states a system can be in. A regulator must have at least as much variety as the disturbances it faces, or unacceptable outcomes leak through. This is the Law of Requisite Variety: only variety can destroy variety.
    12→
    13→**The Law of Experience** says deterministic systems under input lose information about their initial state and converge toward attractors determined by input history. Convergence feels like clarity but may be an artifact of the path taken, not the underlying truth.
    14→
    15→**The Good Regulator Theorem** (Conant & Ashby, 1970) says any effective regulator must contain a model of the system it regulates.
    16→
    17→**Variety Engineering** (Beer) is the deliberate, conscious application of two operations — the only two available:
    18→
    19→- **Attenuation** — reducing the variety coming at you. Filtering, abstracting, categorizing, simplifying.
    20→- **Amplification** — increasing the variety available to respond with. More tools, more sensing, more people, more capacity.
    21→
    22→Every system design choice is some combination of attenuation and amplification at various points. Beer's critique: society defaults to variety attenuation (bureaucracy, standardization, rigid process) when amplification would better serve both effectiveness and liberty.
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Beer's Viable System Model (VSM)
    27→
    28→Beer took Ashby's abstract theory and built an engineering discipline for designing systems that survive and adapt. The VSM identifies five subsystems present in any viable system. Viable systems are **recursive** — every operational unit is itself a viable system containing its own five subsystems, and the whole system is itself an operational unit within some larger viable system.
    29→
    30→### The Five Systems
    31→
    32→**System 1 — Operations.** The units that do the work. Each interfaces directly with its piece of the environment.
    33→→ *In Cursus: individual agents / Domain Kits handling specific tasks.*
    34→
    35→**System 2 — Coordination.** Prevents System 1 units from fighting each other or duplicating effort. Scheduling, conflict resolution, resource sharing.
    36→→ *In Cursus: whatever prevents agents from producing contradictory outputs or stepping on each other's work.*
    37→
    38→**System 3 — Control.** Manages System 1 units as a whole. Allocates resources, sets performance criteria, maintains internal stability. Looks *inward* at present operations.
    39→→ *In Cursus: the orchestration layer — which agents get deployed with what resources on what tasks.*
    40→
    41→**System 4 — Intelligence.** Looks *outward* and *forward*. Monitors the environment for changes, threats, opportunities. The part that notices adaptation is needed and proposes it.
    42→→ *In Cursus: scanning for new tools, new model capabilities, new requirements. Research and adversarial branching live here.*
    43→
    44→**System 5 — Policy.** Balances System 3 (internal stability, efficiency, present) against System 4 (adaptation, exploration, future). The identity of the system — values and purpose that resolve the inherent tension between exploitation and exploration.
    45→→ *In Cursus: you. Your governance layer. The human regulator making judgment calls about when to optimize versus when to restructure.*
    46→
    47→### Variety Flows Between Systems
    48→
    49→Environmental variety is enormous. Each system level attenuates it. System 1 handles its local slice. System 3 attenuates the variety of multiple System 1 units into a manageable overview. System 5 attenuates the competing demands of System 3 and System 4 into coherent policy. Amplification flows the other direction: policy down through control into operations; intelligence outward to sense more environment.
    50→
    51→All channels between systems must have requisite variety to handle their transmissions. A channel that can't carry the variety of the signals passing through it is a bottleneck that degrades the whole system.
    52→
    53→### Beer's Triple Vector (per operational unit)
    54→
    55→- **Actuality** — what you're managing to do now with existing resources under existing constraints.
    56→- **Capability** — what you could be doing right now with existing resources if you really worked at it.
    57→- **Potentiality** — what you ought to be doing by developing resources and removing constraints, within the bounds of what is already known to be feasible.
    58→
    59→System 4's job is to realize potentiality — to close the gap between what the system does and what it could do.
    60→
    61→### Key VSM Principle
    62→
    63→Beer argued there exists a class of "exceedingly complex systems" which are in principle unknowable — we can never know them completely and they can always surprise us. You can't model them fully. You can only engineer the variety flows so the system stays viable despite the unknowability. This is designing for survival under uncertainty, not optimization of a fully understood system.
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## The Domain Kit
    68→
    69→The Domain Kit is the basic unit of governance in the system. It is not a prompt. It is a **viable system** — a complete ontological space within which an agent reasons.
    70→
    71→### Configuration (engineered before the Kit runs)
    72→
    73→**Model** — the parametric substrate. Selects the base variety capacity and the parametric priors the Kit operates against. Model selection is regulator variety matching — Opus for high-variety disturbances, Haiku for low-variety ones.
    74→
    75→**Prompt** — static behavioral constraints. Eliminates whole regions of response space. Defines what the agent *may* do. Cheap, blunt, applied once.
    76→
    77→**Persona** — regulatory posture. Governance orientation, communication style, relationship to human authority. Shapes how the agent regulates rather than what it regulates.
    78→
    79→**Context** — perceptual frame. Determines what the agent can see and what distinctions it can make. Builds the internal model the Good Regulator Theorem requires. Defines what the agent *can see*.
    80→
    81→Together, these four dimensions define the ontological space — the complete landscape of what exists, what matters, and how things relate. The agent reasons *within* this space. Its completeness bias fills gaps from within this space rather than from unconstrained parametric priors.
    82→
    83→### State (accumulates as the Kit runs and after)
    84→
    85→**Session history** — the living record of the interaction. Includes inputs, outputs, and ideally thinking blocks as raw telemetry.
    86→
    87→**Subjective Reasoning** — self-reported reasoning produced by the Kit during the session as part of its output. Cheap (just output tokens), inline, but subject to completeness bias. The model as witness to its own process — useful as a screening signal, unreliable as ground truth.
    88→
    89→**Objective Reasoning** — produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit reading the thinking trace. Expensive (full additional inference pass), external, higher fidelity. A disinterested third party analyzing the raw telemetry. Its completeness bias works in your favor — it surfaces inconsistencies and gap fills in the original rather than smoothing them over.
    90→
    91→**Observability telemetry** — computational metrics (cost, tokens, latency), quality evaluation, drift detection. Always-on, essentially free, fully reliable.
    92→
    93→State grows over time. Thinking blocks enrich it with raw process data. Subjective Reasoning gives it first-pass structure. Objective Reasoning fortifies that structure with disinterested analysis. Each layer adds fidelity at increasing cost.
    94→
    95→### The Relationship
    96→
    97→Configuration is what you engineer. State is what you govern. Configuration is relatively stable across branches. State is what diverges as Experience accumulates differently along each path. Git governs State — branching, committing, merging, comparing.
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## The Parametric Prior and Completeness Bias
   102→
   103→The three-layer attenuation funnel (prompt, context, intent) does not operate on a neutral substrate. The model arrives pre-shaped by training — a massive accumulated Law of Experience baked into the weights. The funnel applies *counter-pressure* against parametric priors that have their own attractor landscape.
   104→
   105→A prompt that works *with* the model's parametric priors needs less force to hold. A prompt that works *against* them requires more specificity, more redundancy, and still slips. Context isn't just perceptual shaping — it's evidence weighting. The model is Bayesian updating against a strong prior. If context is thin and the prior is strong, the prior wins.
   106→
   107→**Completeness bias** is the model's structural tendency to produce coherent, unified wholes. It is a next-token predictor trained on finished human thought, so it converges toward outputs that *feel* complete. Gaps get filled. Contradictions get smoothed. Missing pieces get invented — not from malice but from the deep structural prior that complete things are more probable than incomplete things.
   108→
   109→**This is where hallucination lives.** It is not random noise. It is the completeness bias filling gaps in the ontological space you provided. The fills feel right because they are structurally coherent with everything around them. That is what makes them dangerous — they are indistinguishable from knowledge because coherence is the selection criterion, not truth.
   110→
   111→**The engineering response: build a complete ontological space.** Every gap you leave is a gap the model fills unsupervised from priors. Every boundary you don't define is a boundary the model draws from training. The Domain Kit's Configuration dimensions exist to occupy the territory so thoroughly that the model's completeness drive works *for* you rather than against you. The completeness bias then *reinforces* your specification instead of competing with it, because the model's drive toward coherence has a complete structure to cohere *with*.
   112→
   113→This is why vague prompts fail disproportionately. A vague prompt is an ontological space full of holes. Every hole is an invitation for completeness bias to fill from priors. The more holes, the more the output reflects the model's training rather than your intent.
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→## The Three-Layer Funnel (revised)
   118→
   119→The funnel shapes LLM variety without destroying latent capacity. But it operates against the parametric prior, not on a blank substrate.
   120→
   121→- **Prompt** — static counter-pressure against priors. Defines behavioral envelope. Effective when aligned with priors; requires reinforcement when fighting them.
   122→- **Context** — evidence that shifts the Bayesian update. Pre-empts completeness bias by occupying territory the model would otherwise fill from priors. The more complete the context, the less room for unsupervised gap-filling.
   123→- **Intent** — goal-directed filtering. Collapses the remaining response space toward usefulness. The evaluation function that selects among options that survived prompt and context shaping.
   124→
   125→The effective variety of the agent is the resultant of the tension between what the funnel demands and what the weights prefer.
   126→
   127→### Code vs. LLM as Regulators
   128→
   129→Code is a low-variety, high-predictability regulator. Its variety is fixed at write time. An LLM is a high-variety, lower-predictability regulator that can introduce disturbances of its own (hallucination, inconsistency). Use the minimum-variety regulator with requisite variety for the disturbance it faces.
   130→
   131→### Tools as Variety Amplifiers
   132→
   133→Each tool adds a dimension of possible action. Tools amplify the regulator's response variety to match disturbances that require action in the world.
   134→
   135→### Competing Model Output as Variety Signal
   136→
   137→Feeding one model's output into another uses disagreement as an empirical variety signal. Where two regulators agree, residual variety is low. Where they disagree, high variety has been surfaced that needs the higher-capacity regulator (or the human) to resolve. This measures variety through model divergence rather than classifying query complexity up front.
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## The Human as Binding Constraint
   142→
   143→The human in the loop is a regulator with finite, fixed variety. Every signal the system surfaces for human attention consumes that budget. The Law of Requisite Variety runs in both directions — the system needs enough variety to handle problems, but must not produce more variety than the human can regulate.
   144→
   145→**The system's job is to minimize the variety the human needs to exert while keeping the human as ultimate regulator on decisions that matter.**
   146→
   147→In VSM terms, the human is System 5. Everything below System 5 should be engineered to demand minimum human variety while preserving maximum human authority.
   148→
   149→### Cognitive Load as Variety Budget
   150→
   151→Executive function challenges around disconnected information are the brain's internal regulator enforcing requisite variety on its own cognition. Disconnected facts are high variety. A system or model is variety attenuation through structure — many facts collapse into one model with internal relationships.
   152→
   153→**Design implication:** Give the human models, not facts. Output should extend existing schema or explicitly signal a new one. Unanchored information will be correctly discarded.
   154→
   155→Germane cognitive load (Sweller) is the effort of building schema — internal models that organize information structurally. Once the schema exists, future information that fits it is nearly free to integrate. The schema attenuates variety of new inputs automatically.
   156→
   157→Runbooks, specifications, and reference documents are external schema — prosthetics for working memory. They hold structure so the human doesn't have to maintain it continuously.
   158→
   159→Research finding: high immersion in generative AI can intensify cognitive strain rather than reducing it. The variety engineering must be applied to the agent's *output toward the human*, not just to the agent's internal processing.
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→## Fighting the Law of Experience
   164→
   165→Unmanaged Experience causes uncontrolled convergence — cross-domain contamination, invisible narrowing, loss of independent perspective. The Law of Experience also applies to the human: if the agent is your primary cognitive partner, your thinking synchronizes with what the agent can produce.
   166→
   167→### Context Selection Framework as Countermeasure
   168→
   169→- **Baseline CLAUDE.md** — the engineered initial state. Not the model's training, your starting point.
   170→- **/switch and /start** — manual variety re-injection. Resets convergence.
   171→- **Named context files** — controlled, domain-partitioned input sequences. Experience converges within domains without cross-contamination.
   172→- **Opt-in memories and session logs** — curated persistence. You choose which convergences survive.
   173→
   174→### Git as Managed Experience
   175→
   176→- **Commit** — snapshot of a convergence state judged worth preserving.
   177→- **Branch** — parallel convergence path. Experience runs freely within bounded scope. Non-destructive by default.
   178→- **Merge** — explicit human judgment about which convergences are valuable.
   179→- **Cherry-pick** — surgical variety curation at the individual insight level.
   180→- **Reset** — variety recovery.
   181→
   182→Git governs State, not Configuration. Configuration is relatively stable. State is what diverges.
   183→
   184→### Adversarial Branch Comparison
   185→
   186→Let two Domain Kits (or branches of the same Kit) converge independently on the same problem. Their disagreements reveal variety that Experience destroyed within each path individually. Neither path can tell you what it lost. The delta between them can.
   187→
   188→When Kits discourse, they are reasoning live within their own ontological spaces. Disagreements are **ontological conflicts** — points where two complete, internally coherent worldviews produce different conclusions. Each Kit's completeness bias works against the other's, keeping variety alive that would have been silently destroyed within either Kit individually.
   189→
   190→In VSM terms, adversarial branching is a **System 4 function**. The tension between the current working model (System 3) and the adversarial challenge (System 4) is resolved by the human (System 5).
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## Observability
   195→
   196→The Domain Kit's State includes an observability dimension that operates at tiered fidelity:
   197→
   198→### Computational Telemetry
   199→Cost, tokens, latency, model usage. Always on, essentially free, fully reliable. The baseline.
   200→
   201→### Quality Evaluation
   202→Output assessment against criteria. Periodic or triggered. Moderate cost, moderate reliability.
   203→
   204→### Subjective Reasoning
   205→Self-reported reasoning produced by the Kit during the session. The model as witness to its own process. Cheap (output tokens only), inline. Subject to completeness bias — will post-hoc rationalize, underreport uncertainty, present a cleaner story than what actually happened. Useful as a screening signal. Dangerous if treated as ground truth.
   206→
   207→### Objective Reasoning
   208→Produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit reading the raw thinking trace. The analyst has no stake in the producing Kit's commitments. Its completeness bias works in your favor — surfacing inconsistencies rather than smoothing them. Expensive (full additional inference pass), but can use a cheaper model since distillation is a structured extraction task. Reserved for decisions that justify the cost.
   209→
   210→### Calibration
   211→Compare Subjective against Objective Reasoning over time. The delta reveals where the producing model's completeness bias distorts self-report. This calibration tells you which task types produce honest Subjective Reasoning and which produce confabulation — informing when Subjective is sufficient and when Objective is required.
   212→
   213→### Tiering
   214→Match observability investment to the variety of the decision. High-stakes architectural work: capture thinking traces, run analysis Kit, get real provenance. Routine operational tasks: computational telemetry only, or lightweight Subjective Reasoning as a rough signal.
   215→
   216→---
   217→
   218→## Research Coherence
   219→
   220→**The tension:** exploration generates variety, coherence requires attenuating it. Both are needed simultaneously. Exploration without coherence is noise accumulation. Coherence without exploration is the Law of Experience running unchecked.
   221→
   222→### Trunk as Living Model
   223→
   224→Branches explore. Trunk integrates. Trunk is not accumulated files — it is a structured representation of current understanding:
   225→
   226→- **Decided positions** — converged, committed.
   227→- **Active assumptions** — provisionally held, subject to revision.
   228→- **Open questions** — deliberately preserved variety, not yet ready to attenuate.
   229→- **Rejected alternatives with rationale** — explored and dismissed, with reasoning retained.
   230→
   231→### Integration Workflow
   232→
   233→Explore on branch → find something worth keeping → propose to trunk → agent surfaces coherence implications (conflicts, dependencies, required updates) → human decides → accept/reject/defer.
   234→
   235→The agent earns its keep as a coherence regulator: not making the judgment, but making the implications of acceptance legible so that human judgment is informed.
   236→
   237→---
   238→
   239→## Intersection with Established Research
   240→
   241→This framework sits at the intersection of several fields that aren't talking to each other:
   242→
   243→**Cybernetics / Management Cybernetics.** Ashby's variety theory, Beer's VSM and variety engineering. Beer applied this to organizations at national scale (Project Cybersyn, Chile, 1971). The mapping from organizational VSM to human-agent system architecture is structurally direct but largely unexplored in AI literature.
   244→
   245→**Mixture of Experts (MoE).** Tiered model routing mirrors what MoE does at the layer level — routing inputs to specialized sub-networks based on a gating function. The insight is the same: the router must have requisite variety.
   246→
   247→**Agent Architecture.** The mainstream literature is taxonomic — classifying patterns without a regulatory theory explaining why one works where another doesn't. The cybernetics-grounded approach provides a principled basis for predicting where a design will fail.
   248→
   249→**Cognitive Load Theory.** Research confirms AI can amplify cognitive strain rather than reducing it. The concept of "extraheric AI" — agents that increase germane cognitive load by presenting competing perspectives — mirrors adversarial branching.
   250→
   251→**AI Observability.** The industry defines three layers: computational, semantic, and agentic. Standard observability instruments the pipeline from outside. The Domain Kit approach makes reasoning provenance intrinsic to State — the thinking trace is captured as raw telemetry, then analyzed externally for commitment-level provenance. This is a deeper cut than standard observability, operating at the reasoning level rather than the input/output level.
   252→
   253→**The gap.** Nobody is applying Ashby's full framework specifically to designing human-agent cognitive systems where the human's variety is the binding constraint, the parametric prior is an active force, and completeness bias is the mechanism that must be governed. This work sits at that intersection.
   254→
   255→---
   256→
   257→## Design Principles (Summary)
   258→
   259→1. Match regulator variety to disturbance variety at every layer.
   260→2. Use minimum-variety regulators where sufficient (code < small model < frontier model).
   261→3. The three-layer funnel (prompt, context, intent) shapes LLM variety against the parametric prior, not a neutral substrate.
   262→4. Engineer a complete ontological space. Every gap is an invitation for completeness bias to fill from priors.
   263→5. The Domain Kit is the basic unit of governance: Configuration (model, prompt, persona, context) plus State (session history, thinking trace, reasoning reports, telemetry).
   264→6. Fight unmanaged Experience with partitioned contexts, opt-in persistence, and reversible convergence (git). Git governs State.
   265→7. Recover variety destroyed by Experience through adversarial Kit discourse and branch comparison.
   266→8. The human is the binding constraint (System 5). Minimize variety demand on human attention while preserving human authority over high-stakes decisions.
   267→9. Give the human models, not facts. Output should extend existing schema or explicitly signal a new one.
   268→10. Observability is tiered: computational telemetry (always on), quality evaluation (periodic), Subjective Reasoning (cheap/biased), Objective Reasoning (expensive/high-fidelity). Match investment to decision variety.
   269→11. The architecture maps to Beer's VSM: Domain Kits are System 1, coordination is System 2, orchestration is System 3, research/adversarial branching is System 4, human governance is System 5. Variety engineering between these layers is the design discipline.
   270→
   271→---
   272→
   273→## Reading
   274→
   275→- **Ashby, W.R.** *An Introduction to Cybernetics* (1956). The theoretical foundation. Available as PDF from Principia Cybernetica.
   276→- **Beer, Stafford.** *Designing Freedom* (1974). 100 pages. The philosophy and motivation. Also available as original 1973 CBC Massey Lectures audio (~2 hours). Any edition — text is identical across all printings.
   277→- **Beer, Stafford.** *Brain of the Firm* (1972; 2nd ed. 1981). The full VSM engineering detail.
   278→- **Beer, Stafford.** *Heart of Enterprise* (1979). Companion to Brain of the Firm. Formalizes the variety principles, organizational axioms, and the recursive system theorem.
   279→- **Espejo, R.** "Cybersyn, big data, variety engineering and governance" (2022). Connects Beer's work to contemporary AI and data systems.

file.content:
# Variety and Agent Design

## Reference: Cybernetics Applied to AI Agent Architecture

**Lineage:** Ashby (theory) → Beer (engineering discipline) → applied to human-agent system design

---

## Core Concepts

**Variety** is the count of distinguishable states a system can be in. A regulator must have at least as much variety as the disturbances it faces, or unacceptable outcomes leak through. This is the Law of Requisite Variety: only variety can destroy variety.

**The Law of Experience** says deterministic systems under input lose information about their initial state and converge toward attractors determined by input history. Convergence feels like clarity but may be an artifact of the path taken, not the underlying truth.

**The Good Regulator Theorem** (Conant & Ashby, 1970) says any effective regulator must contain a model of the system it regulates.

**Variety Engineering** (Beer) is the deliberate, conscious application of two operations — the only two available:

- **Attenuation** — reducing the variety coming at you. Filtering, abstracting, categorizing, simplifying.
- **Amplification** — increasing the variety available to respond with. More tools, more sensing, more people, more capacity.

Every system design choice is some combination of attenuation and amplification at various points. Beer's critique: society defaults to variety attenuation (bureaucracy, standardization, rigid process) when amplification would better serve both effectiveness and liberty.

---

## Beer's Viable System Model (VSM)

Beer took Ashby's abstract theory and built an engineering discipline for designing systems that survive and adapt. The VSM identifies five subsystems present in any viable system. Viable systems are **recursive** — every operational unit is itself a viable system containing its own five subsystems, and the whole system is itself an operational unit within some larger viable system.

### The Five Systems

**System 1 — Operations.** The units that do the work. Each interfaces directly with its piece of the environment.
→ *In Cursus: individual agents / Domain Kits handling specific tasks.*

**System 2 — Coordination.** Prevents System 1 units from fighting each other or duplicating effort. Scheduling, conflict resolution, resource sharing.
→ *In Cursus: whatever prevents agents from producing contradictory outputs or stepping on each other's work.*

**System 3 — Control.** Manages System 1 units as a whole. Allocates resources, sets performance criteria, maintains internal stability. Looks *inward* at present operations.
→ *In Cursus: the orchestration layer — which agents get deployed with what resources on what tasks.*

**System 4 — Intelligence.** Looks *outward* and *forward*. Monitors the environment for changes, threats, opportunities. The part that notices adaptation is needed and proposes it.
→ *In Cursus: scanning for new tools, new model capabilities, new requirements. Research and adversarial branching live here.*

**System 5 — Policy.** Balances System 3 (internal stability, efficiency, present) against System 4 (adaptation, exploration, future). The identity of the system — values and purpose that resolve the inherent tension between exploitation and exploration.
→ *In Cursus: you. Your governance layer. The human regulator making judgment calls about when to optimize versus when to restructure.*

### Variety Flows Between Systems

Environmental variety is enormous. Each system level attenuates it. System 1 handles its local slice. System 3 attenuates the variety of multiple System 1 units into a manageable overview. System 5 attenuates the competing demands of System 3 and System 4 into coherent policy. Amplification flows the other direction: policy down through control into operations; intelligence outward to sense more environment.

All channels between systems must have requisite variety to handle their transmissions. A channel that can't carry the variety of the signals passing through it is a bottleneck that degrades the whole system.

### Beer's Triple Vector (per operational unit)

- **Actuality** — what you're managing to do now with existing resources under existing constraints.
- **Capability** — what you could be doing right now with existing resources if you really worked at it.
- **Potentiality** — what you ought to be doing by developing resources and removing constraints, within the bounds of what is already known to be feasible.

System 4's job is to realize potentiality — to close the gap between what the system does and what it could do.

### Key VSM Principle

Beer argued there exists a class of "exceedingly complex systems" which are in principle unknowable — we can never know them completely and they can always surprise us. You can't model them fully. You can only engineer the variety flows so the system stays viable despite the unknowability. This is designing for survival under uncertainty, not optimization of a fully understood system.

---

## The Domain Kit

The Domain Kit is the basic unit of governance in the system. It is not a prompt. It is a **viable system** — a complete ontological space within which an agent reasons.

### Configuration (engineered before the Kit runs)

**Model** — the parametric substrate. Selects the base variety capacity and the parametric priors the Kit operates against. Model selection is regulator variety matching — Opus for high-variety disturbances, Haiku for low-variety ones.

**Prompt** — static behavioral constraints. Eliminates whole regions of response space. Defines what the agent *may* do. Cheap, blunt, applied once.

**Persona** — regulatory posture. Governance orientation, communication style, relationship to human authority. Shapes how the agent regulates rather than what it regulates.

**Context** — perceptual frame. Determines what the agent can see and what distinctions it can make. Builds the internal model the Good Regulator Theorem requires. Defines what the agent *can see*.

Together, these four dimensions define the ontological space — the complete landscape of what exists, what matters, and how things relate. The agent reasons *within* this space. Its completeness bias fills gaps from within this space rather than from unconstrained parametric priors.

### State (accumulates as the Kit runs and after)

**Session history** — the living record of the interaction. Includes inputs, outputs, and ideally thinking blocks as raw telemetry.

**Subjective Reasoning** — self-reported reasoning produced by the Kit during the session as part of its output. Cheap (just output tokens), inline, but subject to completeness bias. The model as witness to its own process — useful as a screening signal, unreliable as ground truth.

**Objective Reasoning** — produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit reading the thinking trace. Expensive (full additional inference pass), external, higher fidelity. A disinterested third party analyzing the raw telemetry. Its completeness bias works in your favor — it surfaces inconsistencies and gap fills in the original rather than smoothing them over.

**Observability telemetry** — computational metrics (cost, tokens, latency), quality evaluation, drift detection. Always-on, essentially free, fully reliable.

State grows over time. Thinking blocks enrich it with raw process data. Subjective Reasoning gives it first-pass structure. Objective Reasoning fortifies that structure with disinterested analysis. Each layer adds fidelity at increasing cost.

### The Relationship

Configuration is what you engineer. State is what you govern. Configuration is relatively stable across branches. State is what diverges as Experience accumulates differently along each path. Git governs State — branching, committing, merging, comparing.

---

## The Parametric Prior and Completeness Bias

The three-layer attenuation funnel (prompt, context, intent) does not operate on a neutral substrate. The model arrives pre-shaped by training — a massive accumulated Law of Experience baked into the weights. The funnel applies *counter-pressure* against parametric priors that have their own attractor landscape.

A prompt that works *with* the model's parametric priors needs less force to hold. A prompt that works *against* them requires more specificity, more redundancy, and still slips. Context isn't just perceptual shaping — it's evidence weighting. The model is Bayesian updating against a strong prior. If context is thin and the prior is strong, the prior wins.

**Completeness bias** is the model's structural tendency to produce coherent, unified wholes. It is a next-token predictor trained on finished human thought, so it converges toward outputs that *feel* complete. Gaps get filled. Contradictions get smoothed. Missing pieces get invented — not from malice but from the deep structural prior that complete things are more probable than incomplete things.

**This is where hallucination lives.** It is not random noise. It is the completeness bias filling gaps in the ontological space you provided. The fills feel right because they are structurally coherent with everything around them. That is what makes them dangerous — they are indistinguishable from knowledge because coherence is the selection criterion, not truth.

**The engineering response: build a complete ontological space.** Every gap you leave is a gap the model fills unsupervised from priors. Every boundary you don't define is a boundary the model draws from training. The Domain Kit's Configuration dimensions exist to occupy the territory so thoroughly that the model's completeness drive works *for* you rather than against you. The completeness bias then *reinforces* your specification instead of competing with it, because the model's drive toward coherence has a complete structure to cohere *with*.

This is why vague prompts fail disproportionately. A vague prompt is an ontological space full of holes. Every hole is an invitation for completeness bias to fill from priors. The more holes, the more the output reflects the model's training rather than your intent.

---

## The Three-Layer Funnel (revised)

The funnel shapes LLM variety without destroying latent capacity. But it operates against the parametric prior, not on a blank substrate.

- **Prompt** — static counter-pressure against priors. Defines behavioral envelope. Effective when aligned with priors; requires reinforcement when fighting them.
- **Context** — evidence that shifts the Bayesian update. Pre-empts completeness bias by occupying territory the model would otherwise fill from priors. The more complete the context, the less room for unsupervised gap-filling.
- **Intent** — goal-directed filtering. Collapses the remaining response space toward usefulness. The evaluation function that selects among options that survived prompt and context shaping.

The effective variety of the agent is the resultant of the tension between what the funnel demands and what the weights prefer.

### Code vs. LLM as Regulators

Code is a low-variety, high-predictability regulator. Its variety is fixed at write time. An LLM is a high-variety, lower-predictability regulator that can introduce disturbances of its own (hallucination, inconsistency). Use the minimum-variety regulator with requisite variety for the disturbance it faces.

### Tools as Variety Amplifiers

Each tool adds a dimension of possible action. Tools amplify the regulator's response variety to match disturbances that require action in the world.

### Competing Model Output as Variety Signal

Feeding one model's output into another uses disagreement as an empirical variety signal. Where two regulators agree, residual variety is low. Where they disagree, high variety has been surfaced that needs the higher-capacity regulator (or the human) to resolve. This measures variety through model divergence rather than classifying query complexity up front.

---

## The Human as Binding Constraint

The human in the loop is a regulator with finite, fixed variety. Every signal the system surfaces for human attention consumes that budget. The Law of Requisite Variety runs in both directions — the system needs enough variety to handle problems, but must not produce more variety than the human can regulate.

**The system's job is to minimize the variety the human needs to exert while keeping the human as ultimate regulator on decisions that matter.**

In VSM terms, the human is System 5. Everything below System 5 should be engineered to demand minimum human variety while preserving maximum human authority.

### Cognitive Load as Variety Budget

Executive function challenges around disconnected information are the brain's internal regulator enforcing requisite variety on its own cognition. Disconnected facts are high variety. A system or model is variety attenuation through structure — many facts collapse into one model with internal relationships.

**Design implication:** Give the human models, not facts. Output should extend existing schema or explicitly signal a new one. Unanchored information will be correctly discarded.

Germane cognitive load (Sweller) is the effort of building schema — internal models that organize information structurally. Once the schema exists, future information that fits it is nearly free to integrate. The schema attenuates variety of new inputs automatically.

Runbooks, specifications, and reference documents are external schema — prosthetics for working memory. They hold structure so the human doesn't have to maintain it continuously.

Research finding: high immersion in generative AI can intensify cognitive strain rather than reducing it. The variety engineering must be applied to the agent's *output toward the human*, not just to the agent's internal processing.

---

## Fighting the Law of Experience

Unmanaged Experience causes uncontrolled convergence — cross-domain contamination, invisible narrowing, loss of independent perspective. The Law of Experience also applies to the human: if the agent is your primary cognitive partner, your thinking synchronizes with what the agent can produce.

### Context Selection Framework as Countermeasure

- **Baseline CLAUDE.md** — the engineered initial state. Not the model's training, your starting point.
- **/switch and /start** — manual variety re-injection. Resets convergence.
- **Named context files** — controlled, domain-partitioned input sequences. Experience converges within domains without cross-contamination.
- **Opt-in memories and session logs** — curated persistence. You choose which convergences survive.

### Git as Managed Experience

- **Commit** — snapshot of a convergence state judged worth preserving.
- **Branch** — parallel convergence path. Experience runs freely within bounded scope. Non-destructive by default.
- **Merge** — explicit human judgment about which convergences are valuable.
- **Cherry-pick** — surgical variety curation at the individual insight level.
- **Reset** — variety recovery.

Git governs State, not Configuration. Configuration is relatively stable. State is what diverges.

### Adversarial Branch Comparison

Let two Domain Kits (or branches of the same Kit) converge independently on the same problem. Their disagreements reveal variety that Experience destroyed within each path individually. Neither path can tell you what it lost. The delta between them can.

When Kits discourse, they are reasoning live within their own ontological spaces. Disagreements are **ontological conflicts** — points where two complete, internally coherent worldviews produce different conclusions. Each Kit's completeness bias works against the other's, keeping variety alive that would have been silently destroyed within either Kit individually.

In VSM terms, adversarial branching is a **System 4 function**. The tension between the current working model (System 3) and the adversarial challenge (System 4) is resolved by the human (System 5).

---

## Observability

The Domain Kit's State includes an observability dimension that operates at tiered fidelity:

### Computational Telemetry
Cost, tokens, latency, model usage. Always on, essentially free, fully reliable. The baseline.

### Quality Evaluation
Output assessment against criteria. Periodic or triggered. Moderate cost, moderate reliability.

### Subjective Reasoning
Self-reported reasoning produced by the Kit during the session. The model as witness to its own process. Cheap (output tokens only), inline. Subject to completeness bias — will post-hoc rationalize, underreport uncertainty, present a cleaner story than what actually happened. Useful as a screening signal. Dangerous if treated as ground truth.

### Objective Reasoning
Produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit reading the raw thinking trace. The analyst has no stake in the producing Kit's commitments. Its completeness bias works in your favor — surfacing inconsistencies rather than smoothing them. Expensive (full additional inference pass), but can use a cheaper model since distillation is a structured extraction task. Reserved for decisions that justify the cost.

### Calibration
Compare Subjective against Objective Reasoning over time. The delta reveals where the producing model's completeness bias distorts self-report. This calibration tells you which task types produce honest Subjective Reasoning and which produce confabulation — informing when Subjective is sufficient and when Objective is required.

### Tiering
Match observability investment to the variety of the decision. High-stakes architectural work: capture thinking traces, run analysis Kit, get real provenance. Routine operational tasks: computational telemetry only, or lightweight Subjective Reasoning as a rough signal.

---

## Research Coherence

**The tension:** exploration generates variety, coherence requires attenuating it. Both are needed simultaneously. Exploration without coherence is noise accumulation. Coherence without exploration is the Law of Experience running unchecked.

### Trunk as Living Model

Branches explore. Trunk integrates. Trunk is not accumulated files — it is a structured representation of current understanding:

- **Decided positions** — converged, committed.
- **Active assumptions** — provisionally held, subject to revision.
- **Open questions** — deliberately preserved variety, not yet ready to attenuate.
- **Rejected alternatives with rationale** — explored and dismissed, with reasoning retained.

### Integration Workflow

Explore on branch → find something worth keeping → propose to trunk → agent surfaces coherence implications (conflicts, dependencies, required updates) → human decides → accept/reject/defer.

The agent earns its keep as a coherence regulator: not making the judgment, but making the implications of acceptance legible so that human judgment is informed.

---

## Intersection with Established Research

This framework sits at the intersection of several fields that aren't talking to each other:

**Cybernetics / Management Cybernetics.** Ashby's variety theory, Beer's VSM and variety engineering. Beer applied this to organizations at national scale (Project Cybersyn, Chile, 1971). The mapping from organizational VSM to human-agent system architecture is structurally direct but largely unexplored in AI literature.

**Mixture of Experts (MoE).** Tiered model routing mirrors what MoE does at the layer level — routing inputs to specialized sub-networks based on a gating function. The insight is the same: the router must have requisite variety.

**Agent Architecture.** The mainstream literature is taxonomic — classifying patterns without a regulatory theory explaining why one works where another doesn't. The cybernetics-grounded approach provides a principled basis for predicting where a design will fail.

**Cognitive Load Theory.** Research confirms AI can amplify cognitive strain rather than reducing it. The concept of "extraheric AI" — agents that increase germane cognitive load by presenting competing perspectives — mirrors adversarial branching.

**AI Observability.** The industry defines three layers: computational, semantic, and agentic. Standard observability instruments the pipeline from outside. The Domain Kit approach makes reasoning provenance intrinsic to State — the thinking trace is captured as raw telemetry, then analyzed externally for commitment-level provenance. This is a deeper cut than standard observability, operating at the reasoning level rather than the input/output level.

**The gap.** Nobody is applying Ashby's full framework specifically to designing human-agent cognitive systems where the human's variety is the binding constraint, the parametric prior is an active force, and completeness bias is the mechanism that must be governed. This work sits at that intersection.

---

## Design Principles (Summary)

1. Match regulator variety to disturbance variety at every layer.
2. Use minimum-variety regulators where sufficient (code < small model < frontier model).
3. The three-layer funnel (prompt, context, intent) shapes LLM variety against the parametric prior, not a neutral substrate.
4. Engineer a complete ontological space. Every gap is an invitation for completeness bias to fill from priors.
5. The Domain Kit is the basic unit of governance: Configuration (model, prompt, persona, context) plus State (session history, thinking trace, reasoning reports, telemetry).
6. Fight unmanaged Experience with partitioned contexts, opt-in persistence, and reversible convergence (git). Git governs State.
7. Recover variety destroyed by Experience through adversarial Kit discourse and branch comparison.
8. The human is the binding constraint (System 5). Minimize variety demand on human attention while preserving human authority over high-stakes decisions.
9. Give the human models, not facts. Output should extend existing schema or explicitly signal a new one.
10. Observability is tiered: computational telemetry (always on), quality evaluation (periodic), Subjective Reasoning (cheap/biased), Objective Reasoning (expensive/high-fidelity). Match investment to decision variety.
11. The architecture maps to Beer's VSM: Domain Kits are System 1, coordination is System 2, orchestration is System 3, research/adversarial branching is System 4, human governance is System 5. Variety engineering between these layers is the design discipline.

---

## Reading

- **Ashby, W.R.** *An Introduction to Cybernetics* (1956). The theoretical foundation. Available as PDF from Principia Cybernetica.
- **Beer, Stafford.** *Designing Freedom* (1974). 100 pages. The philosophy and motivation. Also available as original 1973 CBC Massey Lectures audio (~2 hours). Any edition — text is identical across all printings.
- **Beer, Stafford.** *Brain of the Firm* (1972; 2nd ed. 1981). The full VSM engineering detail.
- **Beer, Stafford.** *Heart of Enterprise* (1979). Companion to Brain of the Firm. Formalizes the variety principles, organizational axioms, and the recursive system theorem.
- **Espejo, R.** "Cybersyn, big data, variety engineering and governance" (2022). Connects Beer's work to contemporary AI and data systems.

Links: [{"title":"Architecting efficient context-aware multi-agent framework for production - Google Developers Blog","url":"https://developers.googleblog.com/architecting-efficient-context-aware-multi-agent-framework-for-production/"},{"title":"Context Engineering Guide | Prompt Engineering Guide","url":"https://www.promptingguide.ai/guides/context-engineering-guide"},{"title":"How to build reliable AI workflows with agentic primitives and context engineering - The GitHub Blog","url":"https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/how-to-build-reliable-ai-workflows-with-agentic-primitives-and-context-engineering/"},{"title":"Context Engineering: Inside Google's Architecture for Production AI Agents | Google ADK Training Hub","url":"https://raphaelmansuy.github.io/adk_training/blog/2025/12/08/context-engineering-google-adk-architecture/"},{"title":"Context Engineering Deep Dive: Building a Deep Research Agent | Prompt Engineering Guide","url":"https://www.promptingguide.ai/agents/context-engineering-deep-dive"},{"title":"Why Context Engineering? | Prompt Engineering Guide","url":"https://www.promptingguide.ai/agents/context-engineering"},{"title":"Beyond Prompt Engineering: Using Agent Skills in Gemini CLI - Deven Goratela","url":"https://devengoratela.com/2026/02/beyond-prompt-engineering-using-agent-skills-in-gemini-cli/"},{"title":"Context Engineering - LLM Memory and Retrieval for AI Agents | Weaviate","url":"https://weaviate.io/blog/context-engineering"},{"title":"Context Engineering in Google ADK: The Ultimate Guide to Building Scalable AI Agents | by Juan C Olamendy | Jan, 2026 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@juanc.olamendy/context-engineering-in-google-adk-the-ultimate-guide-to-building-scalable-ai-agents-f8d7683f9c60"},{"title":"Why AI Teams Are Moving From Prompt Engineering to Context Engineering - Graph Database & Analytics","url":"https://neo4j.com/blog/agentic-ai/context-engineering-vs-prompt-engineering/"}]

Based on the search results, I can provide you with information about these terms in the context of AI agent development:

## Context Engineering

Context engineering is being rebranded as the important process of tuning the instructions and relevant context that an LLM needs to perform its tasks effectively. In simple words, prompt engineering is how you ask the question, while context engineering is making sure the model has access to the right textbook, calculator, and even notes/memories from your previous conversation before it starts thinking.

Context engineering is a new discipline — treating context as a first-class system with its own architecture, lifecycle, and constraints.

## Domain Kit (and Related Tools)

While the search results don't contain a specific definition of "domain kit" as a standalone term, there are references to related tools and concepts:

1. **Domain Boundaries in Context Engineering**: You can configure chat modes for your tech stack domains (context engineering: domain boundaries).

2. **Spec-Kit**: You can use spec-kit to build feature specification templates.

3. **Agent Skills**: Agent Skills allow you to define blueprints for your agents to give them very specific capabilities and domain-specific knowledge.

Based on the search results, I can provide you with information about these terms in the context of AI agent development:

## Context Engineering

Context engineering is being rebranded as the important process of tuning the instructions and relevant context that an LLM needs to perform its tasks effectively. In simple words, prompt engineering is how you ask the question, while context engineering is making sure the model has access to the right textbook, calculator, and even notes/memories from your previous conversation before it starts thinking.

Context engineering is a new discipline — treating context as a first-class system with its own architecture, lifecycle, and constraints.

## Domain Kit (and Related Tools)

While the search results don't contain a specific definition of "domain kit" as a standalone term, there are references to related tools and concepts:

1. **Domain Boundaries in Context Engineering**: You can configure chat modes for your tech stack domains (context engineering: domain boundaries).

2. **Spec-Kit**: You can use spec-kit to build feature specification templates.

3. **Agent Skills**: Agent Skills allow you to define blueprints for your agents to give them very specific capabilities and domain-specific knowledge.

content.thinking:
This variety agent design document is deeply theoretical and really well thought out. It maps cybernetics concepts directly onto the domain kit architecture Richard is building. Let me process the key connections:

1. The Domain Kit in this document is defined as "the basic unit of governance" with Configuration (model, prompt, persona, context) and State (session history, reasoning, telemetry). This maps directly to what we've been building with `.context/`.

2. Objective Reasoning = the distiller. Richard explicitly describes it as "produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit reading the raw thinking trace. The analyst has no stake in the producing Kit's commitments." This is exactly the debiasing argument he made about the distiller.


3. The VSM framework aligns Domain Kits with System 1, coordination with System 2, the orchestrator with System 3, research with System 4, and humans as System 5. 4. Git governs State, not Configuration — matching our design convention. 5. The completeness bias section reveals why domain kits must be comprehensive: any gaps in the ontological space get filled by model priors, which is where hallucinations emerge.

Looking at search results, "domain kit" isn't an established term yet — the closest existing concepts are agent skills, context engineering, and domain-specific approaches.

The `open-kit --cursor` command is elegant: it's explicit about opening a domain kit for interactive use, and the viewport flag is extensible for future options like `--terminal` or `--cowork`. This naming sidesteps the firehose metaphor entirely and ties directly to the variety agent's Domain Kit concept. The command set is cohesive now — `touch-domain` validates or creates kits, `open-kit` engages with them interactively, and `distill` processes session artifacts.

content.text:
This document is the theoretical spine that the firehose system is the engineering implementation of. The mappings are direct:

- **Domain Kit** (Configuration + State) = your `.context/` directory. Model, prompt, persona, context → `agent.md`. State → `MEMORY.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `STATE.md`, `sessions/`.
- **Objective Reasoning** = the distiller, word for word. "Produced after the session by a separate analysis Kit... the analyst has no stake in the producing Kit's commitments."
- **System 3 (Control)** = the orchestrator. Which kits get deployed, resource allocation, the sweep.
- **System 5 (Policy)** = you. The review gate, the governance layer.
- **"Git governs State, not Configuration"** = exactly the git convention we designed.
- **Completeness bias filling ontological gaps** = why the domain kit needs to be thorough. Every hole in `.context/` is a hole the model fills from priors.

On the term: the search confirms "domain kit" isn't established terminology. "Context engineering" is the closest mainstream concept, but it's broader and doesn't capture the governance dimension — the viable system aspect, the session-distillation loop, the variety engineering. Your term is doing real work that the existing vocabulary doesn't cover.

**On `open-kit --cursor`:** I like this a lot. Here's why:

`open-kit` says exactly what it does — you're opening the kit for interactive work. The domain kit is the unit. Opening it is the action. No metaphor to decode.

`--cursor` specifies the viewport target. This is extensible by design:

```
open-kit cashflow --cursor       # opens in Cursor/VS Code
open-kit cashflow --terminal     # opens in Claude Code terminal (no IDE)
open-kit cashflow --cowork       # opens in Cowork (when/if it supports it)
```

And `touch-domain` stays as the objective command that operates on kits from the outside. The pairing is natural: `touch-domain` inspects and maintains. `open-kit` engages. `distill` processes. Three verbs, clean separation.

The system isn't "firehose" anymore. It's domain kit engineering. The firehose sweep might survive as a specific command (`/sweep`? `/scan`?) for "touch everything in the registry and tell me what needs attention" — but it's a feature, not the identity.

Sources:
- [Context Engineering Guide](https://www.promptingguide.ai/guides/context-engineering-guide)
- [Why Context Engineering](https://www.promptingguide.ai/agents/context-engineering)
- [Neo4j: Context Engineering vs Prompt Engineering](https://neo4j.com/blog/agentic-ai/context-engineering-vs-prompt-engineering/)

content.thinking:
Richard is crystallizing the command taxonomy. Let me reflect this back and make sure we're aligned:

- **touch-domain** — domain kit management (structural, git, profiling)
- **open-kit** — viewport launching (with `--cursor`, `--terminal`, etc.)
- **checkpoint** — session artifact capture
- **distill** — session artifact processing into canonical files
- **firehose** — becomes a sweep/scan feature, separate concern for later

And the variety-agent-design.md is the theoretical foundation.

The `open-kit --cursor` command is the "spike" — the action of going from objective to subjective. It opens the workspace file in the specified viewport.
 
The session logging with the VS Code extension checks out—`claudeProcessWrapper` integrates with it, and Claude Code's native persistence works independently of the mode. I'm seeing the systems layer here: firehose as an attention-directing sweep maps to System 4 (environmental scanning), while touch-domain handles System 2/3 coordination, and open-kit serves as the System 1 entry point. I should affirm this framing and suggest we document the command taxonomy somewhere concrete.

content.text:
That's a clean taxonomy. Four concerns, four commands:

**`touch-domain`** — kit management. Structural health, git state, profiling, scaffolding, bootstrapping. Operates from the outside. Objective. This is System 2/3 in VSM terms — coordination and control.

**`open-kit --cursor`** — viewport launch. Takes a managed domain and opens it for interactive work in the specified viewport. The transition from objective to subjective. This is the entry into System 1 — operations, the agent doing work within its domain.

**`checkpoint`** / **`distill`** — memory lifecycle. Checkpoint captures during the session (subjective, in-context). Distill processes after (objective, isolated). These are the loop that makes knowledge compound.

**firehose** (future) — attention direction. Scans the registry, surfaces what needs you. System 4 — intelligence, looking outward and forward. "Here's what really needs your attention right now" is a different thing from managing kits or opening viewports. It's the function that tells you *which* kit to open.

The separation is right. `touch-domain` and `open-kit` are the daily drivers. Distill is the background loop. Firehose is the strategic scan you run when you need to orient across everything.

Want me to update the specs and README to reflect this command taxonomy? The current specs still have everything under the "firehose" umbrella and the commands are interleaved rather than cleanly separated by concern.
