#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  ver=$(claude --version 2>&1 || true)
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2. claude --append-system-prompt-file"
echo ""

# Capture help text with timeout — claude --help may block or use a pager
help_out=$(timeout 5 claude --help 2>&1 || true)
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  # Try alternate forms
  help_out=$(timeout 5 claude -h 2>&1 || true)
fi
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  report WARN "claude --help produced no output (may use a pager). Checking flags via man/docs instead."
  # Fall back to checking if the binary accepts the flag without error
  flag_check_mode="probe"
else
  flag_check_mode="grep"
fi

check_flag() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ "$flag_check_mode" = "grep" ]; then
    if echo "$help_out" | grep -q "$flag"; then
      report PASS "$desc"
      return
    fi
  fi
  # Probe: pass the flag with a no-op and see if it errors about the flag itself
  # For flags that need a value, give a dummy
  case "$flag" in
    --append-system-prompt-file|--system-prompt-file)
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag /dev/null -p "x" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    --session-id)
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag test-probe -p "x" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    -p|--print)
      probe_out=$(timeout 10 claude -p "say OK" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
  esac
  if echo "$probe_out" | grep -qi "unknown option\|unrecognized\|invalid.*flag\|not recognized"; then
    report FAIL "$desc — flag not recognized"
  else
    report PASS "$desc (confirmed via probe)"
  fi
}

check_flag "--append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "3. claude -p (headless/print mode)"
echo ""

check_flag "-p" "-p / --print mode available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "4. claude --system-prompt-file (for distiller)"
echo ""

check_flag "--system-prompt-file" "--system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "5. claude --session-id"
echo ""

check_flag "--session-id" "--session-id available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || true)
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI can open files (non-destructive test)"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  # We don't actually open anything — just verify the flag exists
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "open a file"; then
    report PASS "code CLI supports file opening"
  else
    # code --help varies by version, just check it runs
    report PASS "code CLI is functional (file opening assumed)"
  fi
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks"
echo ""

# This can't be verified from CLI alone — it's a VS Code feature.
# We create a test workspace file and note it for manual verification.
test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE
report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test (see below)"
echo "         To verify: run 'code --new-window $test_ws'"
echo "         Then check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
echo "         Should contain: FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net works"

  # Check if we can create bare repos
  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "test -d /mnt/user/git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "/mnt/user/git/ exists on fluffy"
  else
    report FAIL "/mnt/user/git/ not found on fluffy"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net failed (key auth, connectivity, or host)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git availability"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

# Check if the setting exists in VS Code settings
vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper found in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured yet (optional, for session logging)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code settings file not found at $vscode_settings"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

hooks_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks"
claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -d "$hooks_dir" ] || ([ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null); then
  report PASS "Claude Code hooks directory or config exists"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

content.text: Wait — actually the problem is simpler. All the other probes that worked were passing `-p "x"` which gives claude a prompt to respond to and exit. But `--session-id` combined with `-p` should do the same thing. The issue might be that `--session-id` with `-p` actually resumes an interactive session. Let me just bump the timeout and accept this one might be slow, or skip the live probe entirely.

Kill it with `kill %1`. 

The real issue is that probing claude by actually running it is fragile — it spawns processes, does auth, and doesn't always respect `timeout` cleanly. Let me take a different approach entirely: just check what flags the binary supports by looking at its own documentation, not by running it.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  ver=$(claude --version 2>&1 || true)
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2. claude --append-system-prompt-file"
echo ""

# Capture help text with timeout — claude --help may block or use a pager
help_out=$(timeout 5 claude --help 2>&1 || true)
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  # Try alternate forms
  help_out=$(timeout 5 claude -h 2>&1 || true)
fi
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  report WARN "claude --help produced no output (may use a pager). Checking flags via man/docs instead."
  # Fall back to checking if the binary accepts the flag without error
  flag_check_mode="probe"
else
  flag_check_mode="grep"
fi

check_flag() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ "$flag_check_mode" = "grep" ]; then
    if echo "$help_out" | grep -q "$flag"; then
      report PASS "$desc"
      return
    fi
  fi
  # Probe: pass the flag with a no-op and see if it errors about the flag itself
  # For flags that need a value, give a dummy
  case "$flag" in
    --append-system-prompt-file|--system-prompt-file)
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag /dev/null -p "x" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    --session-id)
      # Just pass a bogus flag combo to see if --session-id is recognized
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    -p|--print)
      probe_out=$(timeout 10 claude -p "say OK" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
  esac
  if echo "$probe_out" | grep -qi "unknown option\|unrecognized\|invalid.*flag\|not recognized"; then
    report FAIL "$desc — flag not recognized"
  else
    report PASS "$desc (confirmed via probe)"
  fi
}

check_flag "--append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "3. claude -p (headless/print mode)"
echo ""

check_flag "-p" "-p / --print mode available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "4. claude --system-prompt-file (for distiller)"
echo ""

check_flag "--system-prompt-file" "--system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "5. claude --session-id"
echo ""

check_flag "--session-id" "--session-id available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || true)
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI can open files (non-destructive test)"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  # We don't actually open anything — just verify the flag exists
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "open a file"; then
    report PASS "code CLI supports file opening"
  else
    # code --help varies by version, just check it runs
    report PASS "code CLI is functional (file opening assumed)"
  fi
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks"
echo ""

# This can't be verified from CLI alone — it's a VS Code feature.
# We create a test workspace file and note it for manual verification.
test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE
report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test (see below)"
echo "         To verify: run 'code --new-window $test_ws'"
echo "         Then check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
echo "         Should contain: FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net works"

  # Check if we can create bare repos
  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "test -d /mnt/user/git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "/mnt/user/git/ exists on fluffy"
  else
    report FAIL "/mnt/user/git/ not found on fluffy"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net failed (key auth, connectivity, or host)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git availability"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

# Check if the setting exists in VS Code settings
vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper found in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured yet (optional, for session logging)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code settings file not found at $vscode_settings"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

hooks_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks"
claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -d "$hooks_dir" ] || ([ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null); then
  report PASS "Claude Code hooks directory or config exists"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

content.text: The fundamental problem is clear: invoking `claude` at all from a script is unreliable. It can spawn interactive sessions, hang on auth, ignore signals. Let me rewrite the whole approach — no live `claude` invocations for flag checks. Instead, inspect the binary's own metadata.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively or with probes.
# Claude Code doesn't behave well as a subprocess for flag-testing — it can
# hang on auth, spawn interactive sessions, or ignore SIGINT. Instead we
# check the installed npm package metadata for flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# For flag checks, search the installed claude package source rather than
# invoking the binary. This avoids all the hanging/auth/interactive issues.
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

claude_source=""
# Try to find the installed package source
if [ -n "$claude_bin" ]; then
  # Resolve symlinks to find the actual install
  real_bin=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  pkg_dir=$(dirname "$(dirname "$real_bin")" 2>/dev/null || true)

  # Search common locations for the CLI source
  for candidate in \
    "$pkg_dir" \
    "$HOME/.local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.local/share/npm/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code"; do
    if [ -d "$candidate" ] && find "$candidate" -name "*.js" -maxdepth 3 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -q .; then
      claude_source="$candidate"
      break
    fi
  done
fi

check_flag_in_source() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_source" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — could not locate claude package source"
    return
  fi
  # Search JS source for the flag string
  if grep -r "$flag" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -l -q 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in source (may use different internal name)"
  fi
}

if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  echo "  (checking flags in package source: $claude_source)"
  echo ""
fi

check_flag_in_source "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_source "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_source "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_source '"\-p"\|"print"'            "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# For -p, the grep above is noisy. Do a more targeted check.
if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  if grep -r "print" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -q 2>/dev/null; then
    # -p is a fundamental feature, safe to assume if claude exists
    :
  fi
  # Override: -p is documented and confirmed working from our earlier probe
  # (checks 2-4 confirmed it responds). Just mark it.
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI flags"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

# Check if a previous manual test succeeded
if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   code --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

# Read server from firehose.local.md if available
git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    # Check if firehose.git bare repo exists
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server (create with touch-domain --new)"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# Check if firehose itself is a git repo
if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code user settings not found at expected path"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI flags"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

# Check if a previous manual test succeeded
if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   code --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

# Read server from firehose.local.md if available
git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    # Check if firehose.git bare repo exists
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server (create with touch-domain --new)"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# Check if firehose itself is a git repo
if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code user settings not found at expected path"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  ver=$(claude --version 2>&1 || true)
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2. claude --append-system-prompt-file"
echo ""

# Capture help text with timeout — claude --help may block or use a pager
help_out=$(timeout 5 claude --help 2>&1 || true)
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  # Try alternate forms
  help_out=$(timeout 5 claude -h 2>&1 || true)
fi
if [ -z "$help_out" ]; then
  report WARN "claude --help produced no output (may use a pager). Checking flags via man/docs instead."
  # Fall back to checking if the binary accepts the flag without error
  flag_check_mode="probe"
else
  flag_check_mode="grep"
fi

check_flag() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ "$flag_check_mode" = "grep" ]; then
    if echo "$help_out" | grep -q "$flag"; then
      report PASS "$desc"
      return
    fi
  fi
  # Probe: pass the flag with a no-op and see if it errors about the flag itself
  # For flags that need a value, give a dummy
  case "$flag" in
    --append-system-prompt-file|--system-prompt-file)
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag /dev/null -p "x" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    --session-id)
      # Just pass a bogus flag combo to see if --session-id is recognized
      probe_out=$(timeout 5 claude $flag 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
    -p|--print)
      probe_out=$(timeout 10 claude -p "say OK" 2>&1 || true)
      ;;
  esac
  if echo "$probe_out" | grep -qi "unknown option\|unrecognized\|invalid.*flag\|not recognized"; then
    report FAIL "$desc — flag not recognized"
  else
    report PASS "$desc (confirmed via probe)"
  fi
}

check_flag "--append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "3. claude -p (headless/print mode)"
echo ""

check_flag "-p" "-p / --print mode available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "4. claude --system-prompt-file (for distiller)"
echo ""

check_flag "--system-prompt-file" "--system-prompt-file available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "5. claude --session-id"
echo ""

check_flag "--session-id" "--session-id available"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || true)
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI can open files (non-destructive test)"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  # We don't actually open anything — just verify the flag exists
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "open a file"; then
    report PASS "code CLI supports file opening"
  else
    # code --help varies by version, just check it runs
    report PASS "code CLI is functional (file opening assumed)"
  fi
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks"
echo ""

# This can't be verified from CLI alone — it's a VS Code feature.
# We create a test workspace file and note it for manual verification.
test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE
report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test (see below)"
echo "         To verify: run 'code --new-window $test_ws'"
echo "         Then check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
echo "         Should contain: FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net works"

  # Check if we can create bare repos
  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@fluffy.geekazoid.net "test -d /mnt/user/git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "/mnt/user/git/ exists on fluffy"
  else
    report FAIL "/mnt/user/git/ not found on fluffy"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to fluffy.geekazoid.net failed (key auth, connectivity, or host)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git availability"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

# Check if the setting exists in VS Code settings
vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper found in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured yet (optional, for session logging)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code settings file not found at $vscode_settings"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

hooks_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks"
claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -d "$hooks_dir" ] || ([ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null); then
  report PASS "Claude Code hooks directory or config exists"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

content.text:
Complete rewrite. Key change: **zero `claude` invocations** except `claude --version` (which we know works). For the flag checks, it finds the installed npm package source and greps the JS for the flag strings. Everything else is filesystem checks, `ssh`, and `code --help`.

The `folderOpen` test is now re-runnable — first time it tells you to manually open the workspace, subsequent runs detect the sentinel file and mark it PASS.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively or with probes.
# Claude Code doesn't behave well as a subprocess for flag-testing — it can
# hang on auth, spawn interactive sessions, or ignore SIGINT. Instead we
# check the installed npm package metadata for flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# For flag checks, search the installed claude package source rather than
# invoking the binary. This avoids all the hanging/auth/interactive issues.
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

claude_source=""
# Try to find the installed package source
if [ -n "$claude_bin" ]; then
  # Resolve symlinks to find the actual install
  real_bin=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  pkg_dir=$(dirname "$(dirname "$real_bin")" 2>/dev/null || true)

  # Search common locations for the CLI source
  for candidate in \
    "$pkg_dir" \
    "$HOME/.local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.local/share/npm/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code"; do
    if [ -d "$candidate" ] && find "$candidate" -name "*.js" -maxdepth 3 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -q .; then
      claude_source="$candidate"
      break
    fi
  done
fi

check_flag_in_source() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_source" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — could not locate claude package source"
    return
  fi
  # Search JS source for the flag string
  if grep -r "$flag" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -l -q 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in source (may use different internal name)"
  fi
}

if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  echo "  (checking flags in package source: $claude_source)"
  echo ""
fi

check_flag_in_source "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_source "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_source "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_source '"\-p"\|"print"'            "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# For -p, the grep above is noisy. Do a more targeted check.
if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  if grep -r "print" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -q 2>/dev/null; then
    # -p is a fundamental feature, safe to assume if claude exists
    :
  fi
  # Override: -p is documented and confirmed working from our earlier probe
  # (checks 2-4 confirmed it responds). Just mark it.
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI flags"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

# Check if a previous manual test succeeded
if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   code --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

# Read server from firehose.local.md if available
git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    # Check if firehose.git bare repo exists
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server (create with touch-domain --new)"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# Check if firehose itself is a git repo
if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code user settings not found at expected path"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively. Claude Code hangs on
# auth, spawns interactive sessions, or ignores SIGINT when used as a
# subprocess. Instead we use `strings` on the compiled binary to confirm
# flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
claude_real=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  claude_real=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
  report PASS "binary: $claude_real"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

# Use `strings` on the compiled binary to check for flag support
# without invoking claude interactively.
check_flag_in_binary() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_real" ] || [ ! -f "$claude_real" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — claude binary not found"
    return
  fi
  if strings "$claude_real" | grep -q "$flag"; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in binary strings"
  fi
}

check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary "\-\-print"                 "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. Editor CLI (Cursor / VS Code)"
echo ""

# Prefer cursor, fall back to code
editor_cmd=""
if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="cursor"
  editor_ver=$(cursor --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "cursor CLI found: $editor_ver"
elif command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="code"
  editor_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $editor_ver"
else
  report FAIL "neither cursor nor code CLI found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. Editor CLI flags"
echo ""

if [ -n "$editor_cmd" ]; then
  editor_help=$($editor_cmd --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$editor_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "$editor_cmd --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "$editor_cmd --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "no editor CLI available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. Workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  open_cmd="${editor_cmd:-code}"
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   $open_cmd --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (editor setting)"
echo ""

# Check both Cursor and VS Code settings paths
found_wrapper=false
for settings_path in \
  "$HOME/.config/Cursor/User/settings.json" \
  "$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"; do
  if [ -f "$settings_path" ]; then
    if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$settings_path" 2>/dev/null; then
      report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in $settings_path"
      found_wrapper=true
      break
    fi
  fi
done
if [ "$found_wrapper" = false ]; then
  report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively. Claude Code hangs on
# auth, spawns interactive sessions, or ignores SIGINT when used as a
# subprocess. Instead we use `strings` on the compiled binary to confirm
# flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
claude_real=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  claude_real=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
  report PASS "binary: $claude_real"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

# Use `strings` on the compiled binary to check for flag support
# without invoking claude interactively.
check_flag_in_binary() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_real" ] || [ ! -f "$claude_real" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — claude binary not found"
    return
  fi
  if strings "$claude_real" | grep -q "$flag"; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in binary strings"
  fi
}

check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary "\-\-print"                 "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. Editor CLI (Cursor / VS Code)"
echo ""

# Prefer cursor, fall back to code
editor_cmd=""
if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="cursor"
  editor_ver=$(cursor --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "cursor CLI found: $editor_ver"
elif command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="code"
  editor_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $editor_ver"
else
  report FAIL "neither cursor nor code CLI found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. Editor CLI flags"
echo ""

if [ -n "$editor_cmd" ]; then
  editor_help=$($editor_cmd --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$editor_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "$editor_cmd --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "$editor_cmd --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "no editor CLI available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. Workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  open_cmd="${editor_cmd:-code}"
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   $open_cmd --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (editor setting)"
echo ""

# Check both Cursor and VS Code settings paths
found_wrapper=false
for settings_path in \
  "$HOME/.config/Cursor/User/settings.json" \
  "$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"; do
  if [ -f "$settings_path" ]; then
    if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$settings_path" 2>/dev/null; then
      report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in $settings_path"
      found_wrapper=true
      break
    fi
  fi
done
if [ "$found_wrapper" = false ]; then
  report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively or with probes.
# Claude Code doesn't behave well as a subprocess for flag-testing — it can
# hang on auth, spawn interactive sessions, or ignore SIGINT. Instead we
# check the installed npm package metadata for flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# For flag checks, search the installed claude package source rather than
# invoking the binary. This avoids all the hanging/auth/interactive issues.
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

claude_source=""
# Try to find the installed package source
if [ -n "$claude_bin" ]; then
  # Resolve symlinks to find the actual install
  real_bin=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  pkg_dir=$(dirname "$(dirname "$real_bin")" 2>/dev/null || true)

  # Search common locations for the CLI source
  for candidate in \
    "$pkg_dir" \
    "$HOME/.local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code" \
    "$HOME/.local/share/npm/lib/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code"; do
    if [ -d "$candidate" ] && find "$candidate" -name "*.js" -maxdepth 3 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -q .; then
      claude_source="$candidate"
      break
    fi
  done
fi

check_flag_in_source() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_source" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — could not locate claude package source"
    return
  fi
  # Search JS source for the flag string
  if grep -r "$flag" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -l -q 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in source (may use different internal name)"
  fi
}

if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  echo "  (checking flags in package source: $claude_source)"
  echo ""
fi

check_flag_in_source "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_source "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_source "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_source '"\-p"\|"print"'            "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# For -p, the grep above is noisy. Do a more targeted check.
if [ -n "$claude_source" ]; then
  if grep -r "print" "$claude_source" --include="*.js" -q 2>/dev/null; then
    # -p is a fundamental feature, safe to assume if claude exists
    :
  fi
  # Override: -p is documented and confirmed working from our earlier probe
  # (checks 2-4 confirmed it responds). Just mark it.
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. VS Code / Cursor CLI"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $code_ver"
else
  report FAIL "code CLI not found in PATH"
fi

if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "cursor CLI also available"
else
  report SKIP "cursor CLI not found (not required if using code)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. code CLI flags"
echo ""

if command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  code_help=$(code --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$code_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "code --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "code --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "code CLI not available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. VS Code workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

# Check if a previous manual test succeeded
if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   code --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

# Read server from firehose.local.md if available
git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    # Check if firehose.git bare repo exists
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server (create with touch-domain --new)"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

# Check if firehose itself is a git repo
if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (VS Code setting)"
echo ""

vscode_settings="$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"
if [ -f "$vscode_settings" ]; then
  if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$vscode_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
    report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in VS Code settings"
  else
    report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "VS Code user settings not found at expected path"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

content.text: The strings are there — the issue is grep escaping in the script. The `--print` check uses `\-\-print` which is wrong for `strings | grep`. Let me fix the flag checks:

input.old_string:
check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary "\-\-print"                 "-p / --print (headless mode)"
input.new_string:

check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary -- "--print"                "-p / --print (headless mode)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-assumptions.sh — smoke tests for domain kit runtime assumptions
# Run from the firehose repo root. Reports PASS/FAIL/SKIP for each check.
#
# DESIGN NOTE: We never invoke `claude` interactively. Claude Code hangs on
# auth, spawns interactive sessions, or ignores SIGINT when used as a
# subprocess. Instead we use `strings` on the compiled binary to confirm
# flag support.

set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SKIP=0
WARN=0

report() {
  local status="$1" msg="$2"
  case "$status" in
    PASS) echo -e "  ✓ PASS: $msg"; PASS=$((PASS+1)) ;;
    FAIL) echo -e "  ✗ FAIL: $msg"; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)) ;;
    SKIP) echo -e "  - SKIP: $msg"; SKIP=$((SKIP+1)) ;;
    WARN) echo -e "  ! WARN: $msg"; WARN=$((WARN+1)) ;;
  esac
}

echo "=== Domain Kit Assumption Verification ==="
echo ""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "1. Claude Code CLI"
echo ""

claude_bin=""
claude_real=""
if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  claude_bin=$(command -v claude)
  claude_real=$(readlink -f "$claude_bin" 2>/dev/null || echo "$claude_bin")
  ver=$(timeout 5 claude --version 2>&1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "claude CLI found: $ver"
  report PASS "binary: $claude_real"
else
  report FAIL "claude CLI not found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "2-5. Claude Code CLI flags"
echo ""

# Use `strings` on the compiled binary to check for flag support
# without invoking claude interactively.
check_flag_in_binary() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_real" ] || [ ! -f "$claude_real" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — claude binary not found"
    return
  fi
  if strings "$claude_real" | grep -q "$flag"; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in binary strings"
  fi
}

check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary "\-\-print"                 "-p / --print (headless mode)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "6. Editor CLI (Cursor / VS Code)"
echo ""

# Prefer cursor, fall back to code
editor_cmd=""
if command -v cursor &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="cursor"
  editor_ver=$(cursor --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "cursor CLI found: $editor_ver"
elif command -v code &>/dev/null; then
  editor_cmd="code"
  editor_ver=$(code --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo "unknown")
  report PASS "code CLI found: $editor_ver"
else
  report FAIL "neither cursor nor code CLI found in PATH"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "7. Editor CLI flags"
echo ""

if [ -n "$editor_cmd" ]; then
  editor_help=$($editor_cmd --help 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$editor_help" | grep -q "\-\-new-window"; then
    report PASS "$editor_cmd --new-window flag available"
  else
    report WARN "$editor_cmd --new-window not confirmed in help output"
  fi
else
  report SKIP "no editor CLI available"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "8. Workspace folderOpen tasks (manual)"
echo ""

test_ws="/tmp/firehose-verify-test.code-workspace"
cat > "$test_ws" << 'WORKSPACE'
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "/tmp" }
  ],
  "tasks": {
    "version": "2.0.0",
    "tasks": [
      {
        "label": "Verify folderOpen",
        "type": "shell",
        "command": "echo FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS > /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt",
        "presentation": { "reveal": "silent" },
        "runOptions": { "runOn": "folderOpen" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
WORKSPACE

if [ -f /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt ] && grep -q "FIREHOSE_FOLDEROPEN_WORKS" /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "folderOpen tasks work (confirmed by previous manual test)"
else
  open_cmd="${editor_cmd:-code}"
  report SKIP "folderOpen tasks require manual test"
  echo "         Run:   $open_cmd --new-window $test_ws"
  echo "         Check: cat /tmp/firehose-folderopen-result.txt"
  echo "         Then re-run this script to confirm."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "9. SSH to primary git server"
echo ""

git_host="root@fluffy.geekazoid.net"
git_path="/mnt/user/git"

if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes "$git_host" "echo reachable" 2>/dev/null | grep -q reachable; then
  report PASS "SSH to $git_host works"

  if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
    report PASS "$git_path exists on server"
    if ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 "$git_host" "test -d $git_path/firehose.git && echo exists" 2>/dev/null | grep -q exists; then
      report PASS "firehose.git bare repo exists on server"
    else
      report WARN "firehose.git bare repo not found on server"
    fi
  else
    report FAIL "$git_path not found on server"
  fi
else
  report FAIL "SSH to $git_host failed"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "10. Git"
echo ""

if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  git_ver=$(git --version 2>&1)
  report PASS "$git_ver"
else
  report FAIL "git not found"
fi

if git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "firehose repo is under git"
else
  report WARN "firehose repo is NOT a git repo yet"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "11. claudeProcessWrapper (editor setting)"
echo ""

# Check both Cursor and VS Code settings paths
found_wrapper=false
for settings_path in \
  "$HOME/.config/Cursor/User/settings.json" \
  "$HOME/.config/Code/User/settings.json"; do
  if [ -f "$settings_path" ]; then
    if grep -q "claudeProcessWrapper\|claude.processWrapper" "$settings_path" 2>/dev/null; then
      report PASS "claudeProcessWrapper configured in $settings_path"
      found_wrapper=true
      break
    fi
  fi
done
if [ "$found_wrapper" = false ]; then
  report SKIP "claudeProcessWrapper not configured (optional — for session log capture)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "12. Claude Code hooks"
echo ""

claude_settings="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
if [ -f "$claude_settings" ] && grep -q "hooks" "$claude_settings" 2>/dev/null; then
  report PASS "hooks configured in $claude_settings"
else
  report SKIP "No hooks configured yet (needed for SessionStart auto-context)"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "  PASS: $PASS  |  FAIL: $FAIL  |  WARN: $WARN  |  SKIP: $SKIP"
echo ""

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Some checks failed. Review above for details."
  exit 1
else
  echo "No hard failures. SKIP/WARN items need manual verification or future setup."
  exit 0
fi

content.text: Wait — the issue is in the function itself. `strings "$claude_real" | grep -q "$flag"` — `grep -q` with a pattern starting with `--` would be treated as a flag. And `strings` on the binary might be producing more output than the pipe can handle. Let me look at how the function works:

input.old_string:
check_flag_in_binary() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_real" ] || [ ! -f "$claude_real" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — claude binary not found"
    return
  fi
  if strings "$claude_real" | grep -q "$flag"; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in binary strings"
  fi
}

check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary -- "--print"                "-p / --print (headless mode)"
input.new_string:
check_flag_in_binary() {
  local flag="$1" desc="$2"
  if [ -z "$claude_real" ] || [ ! -f "$claude_real" ]; then
    report SKIP "$desc — claude binary not found"
    return
  fi
  if strings "$claude_real" | grep -qF "$flag"; then
    report PASS "$desc"
  else
    report WARN "$desc — not found in binary strings"
  fi
}

check_flag_in_binary "append-system-prompt-file" "--append-system-prompt-file (agent.md injection)"
check_flag_in_binary "system-prompt-file"        "--system-prompt-file (distiller prompt)"
check_flag_in_binary "session-id"                "--session-id (session tracking)"
check_flag_in_binary "--print"                   "-p / --print (headless mode)"
